# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: '16'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Run unit tests
      run: npm test

    - name: Build application
      run: npm run build

    - name: Deploy to staging
      env:
        STAGING_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STAGING_API_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "Deploying to staging environment"
        # Secure deployment script here, using secure APIs and validated inputs
        # Example: curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $STAGING_API_KEY" https://staging.example.com/deploy

    - name: Clean up
      run: |
        echo "Cleaning up"
        # Remove any temporary files or sensitive data from the runner
        rm -rf ./temp
